title: Odd Jobs & Small Commissions
description: Small, low-stakes jobs that fill downtime, establish local contacts, and occasionally reveal something larger — usable in any genre.

entries.result: Deliver a sealed envelope to an address two districts over. Do not open it. Do not let it get wet.
Locate a missing pet — a large, unfriendly, and apparently talented escape artist.
Guard a merchant's cart for an afternoon while they attend a meeting they won't describe.
Help an elderly resident move furniture from one floor to another before their landlord inspects the property.
Collect an overdue debt from a neighbor. The neighbor is technically right that the debt is ambiguous but socially wrong.
Find out who has been leaving anonymous letters in a local's mailbox. The letters are kind. The recipient doesn't like it.
Escort a nervous student to an exam across a rough part of town.
Retrieve a book from a lender who has had it for eight months and no longer answers the door when the owner knocks.
Babysit three children for an afternoon. The parents emphasize that the children are not as innocent as they appear.
Help prepare for a large family dinner by acquiring several specific ingredients from different parts of the market.
Serve as a witness at a document signing that may or may not be entirely legal.
Find lodging for a traveling family who arrived without a reservation during a busy week.
Translate a document from one language to another. The document is in three languages and none of them fully overlap.
Stand in a long line on behalf of a merchant who has other things to do but cannot lose their place.
Accompany someone to a difficult meeting and be present as moral support and potential deterrence.
Pick up a custom order from a craftsperson who has been avoiding the client's messages.
Find out if a local shop is cheating customers on measurements, and document the evidence.
Help a performer set up for a show and stay on call in case the crowd becomes difficult.
Locate a specific secondhand item at local markets before an upcoming gift-giving occasion.
Carry a message verbally to someone who doesn't trust written communications.
Find a runaway apprentice and return them before their master's patience expires.
Help a local business owner break a lease that's worded to make breaking it difficult.
Investigate why a regular customer has stopped patronizing an establishment they've used for years.
Acquire a specific type of food that can't be found locally. The client will tell you where it might be found but isn't sure.
Test a new product — the manufacturer is vague about the exact parameters of the test.
Help settle an argument between two neighbors that has been escalating for six months over something trivial.
Find a replacement for an item that was broken during an accident that no one wants to admit happened.
Escort a package across town that requires someone to be present with it at all times.
Help an older resident navigate a bureaucratic process they find confusing and that requires several offices.
Verify a story — a local is not sure whether something they were told is true and wants a neutral party to check.
Find a lost item that slipped into the drain, the river, or the unreachable back corner of something.
Remove something from a location that the owner wants gone but doesn't want to touch.
Post a series of notices around the city before the morning crowd arrives.
Convince a stubborn relative to attend a family event they've refused twice.
Sit in at a meeting to take notes and report back accurately and completely.
Recommend a trustworthy tradesperson for a job that several disreputable ones have bid on.
Find out whether a rumor circulating about a specific business is true, false, or partially both.
Help a struggling seller move old stock by finding buyers at a discount.
Cover for an absent employee for a few hours without letting the employer notice they're gone.
Check on a friend of a friend who lives in a rough area and hasn't been responding to messages.
Carry a heavy load that the client is too proud to admit they can't carry.
Find out if a specific shop is open on a holiday when the client can't verify directly.
Help organize an event that has grown past the organizer's ability to manage.
Distract a specific person for an hour while something unrelated happens nearby.
Return borrowed money to someone who didn't ask for a loan and doesn't know they funded one.
Retrieve an item left at a place the client would be recognized and judged for entering.
Secure a location from a recurring trespasser without causing trouble for the client.
Teach a skill to someone with no aptitude and two days to learn it.
Help a client disappear from a social obligation they deeply regret agreeing to.
Hunt down a repair service that has had a client's property for three weeks and stopped answering.
Locate a specific person the client knows only by a description and a neighborhood.
Help clean out a storage space before a deadline with no information about what's in it.
Find a reliable person to fill a position quickly after an unexpected vacancy.
Investigate a noise complaint that the authorities have dismissed twice.
Acquire a gift for someone the client doesn't know well, based on very little information.
Help a street vendor secure a permanent spot they've been holding informally for months.
Spend an afternoon as a second set of eyes for someone who can't trust their own judgment on a purchase.
Take a meal to someone who is too sick to leave their home but too proud to ask for help.
Keep watch on a specific intersection for two hours and report everything that passes.
Pull together information on a person from public records before a business meeting.
Mediate a minor dispute between two people who trust an outside opinion more than each other.
Procure something legal but hard to find in this city, available more easily elsewhere.
Help a craftsperson identify why their work keeps failing at a specific step.
Map out a section of a building the client needs to understand before a meeting there.
Check whether a specific establishment is safe to visit after reports of recent trouble.
Act as a reference for a stranger applying for a position. The stranger was vouched for by someone the party trusts.
Return a runaway mule before it reaches a second town and becomes a jurisdictional problem.
Transcribe an oral story before the teller forgets it. The teller forgets things.
Buy an item at market value from a seller who won't sell to anyone they know personally.
Confirm or deny whether an address still exists at the location listed on an old letter.
Help prepare a memorial for someone whose family is in a different city and can't arrive in time.
Follow a specific route and time it, noting every obstacle along the way.
Retrieve a child's toy from a place the parents can't explain to the child why they won't go.
Help someone compose and send a letter they've been avoiding writing for months.
Stake out a vendor who is suspected of using two different price lists.
Recover a boat that drifted from its mooring and has been spotted three docks down.
Negotiate a refund from a business whose policy technically prohibits refunds.
Find out who broke a window and whether it was intentional.
Help a traveling family navigate a city they've never visited, to a specific address.
Take someone's place at a formal event they're too ill to attend.
Find a quiet place for someone to meet privately in a city where they're recognized.
Remove graffiti before a business opens in the morning.
Help a performer whose instrument is broken find a replacement before tonight's show.
Identify the source of a smell in a shared building that everyone can detect and no one can locate.
Accompany an anxious person to a medical appointment and stay until they're released.
Confirm that a specific item is authentic before a client commits to purchasing it.
Track down a contractor who took a deposit, started the work, and hasn't returned in two weeks.
Find out what a neighbor is building in their yard that has been going on for three weeks at odd hours.
Help a new business owner understand the informal rules of their district.
Check in on an elderly neighbor for a week while the usual checker-in is out of town.
Acquire a rare supply that a local business depends on before a competitor buys it out.
Determine whether a new employee is actually stealing or is simply clumsy.
Help a family relocate in a single day before their current lease terminates.
Find out if a specific rumor started at a specific location and if so, who started it.
Keep a scheduled delivery from arriving at the wrong address without stopping the delivery entirely.
Help someone win a public bet by finding an answer they're certain exists but can't locate.
Pose as a customer to evaluate a competitor's service quality for a local business.
Unblock a doorway that has been obstructed by a neighboring business's storage.
Help a writer's block-afflicted local by finding the story they said they needed and couldn't find.
Do something the client can't explain and won't fully explain — not because it's illegal, but because explaining it would mean admitting they can't handle it themselves.
